title: Extra-Tall Bar Stool
summary: A tall bar stool with a backrest and footrest from 2x2 legs and 2x3 rails — built for high pub-height counters where standard stools come up short.
description:
Standard bar stools top out around 30", which leaves you perched below some tall pub or shop counters. This one runs taller, with 50" back legs that carry a backrest and 33" front legs, plus a footrest rail — the height detail that makes a tall stool actually comfortable, since your feet need somewhere to go.

The build is blunt and strong: predrill, glue, and countersink screws at every joint. Assemble the back (the two long legs plus three 2x3 backrest boards) and the front (the two short legs plus the 2x3 seat-rest and 2x2 footrest) as separate frames, then join them with 2x3 side rails. Doing it as two frames first keeps everything square and manageable.

The seat can be almost anything flat — the source cut solid pine rounds (four seats out of an 18"-wide, 6-foot board), but plywood or glued-up 1x4s work just as well. Because it's a tall stool, stability matters: check it doesn't rack or wobble before you finish it, and make sure all four feet land evenly. Countersink and fill the screw holes for a clean look, then finish.

materials.name: 1/2" solid wood or plywood for seat

cutList.note: Cut a round or square; solid pine, plywood, or glued 1x4s.

steps.title: Build the back
steps.body: Working on a flat surface, assemble the back using the 50" legs. Predrill all holes, glue, and countersink the screws. Attach the three 12.5" 2x3 backrest boards, centered, to form the backrest.

steps.title: Build the front
steps.body: Assemble the front using the 33" legs — predrill, glue, countersink. Attach the 2x3 seat-rest board with a screw toward the top, and add the 2x2 front footrest lower down. The footrest is what makes a tall stool livable.

steps.title: Join front to back
steps.body: Attach the back and front frames together with the 13" 2x3 side seat-rest boards, predrilling, gluing, and countersinking. Check the whole stool for square and that it doesn't rack.

steps.title: Add the seat and finish
steps.body: Cut the seat from a solid board, plywood, or glued-up 1x4s and fasten it on. Confirm all four feet land level. Fill the countersunk screw holes, sand, and finish.
materials: 1/2" solid wood or plywood for seat

images.alt: Extra-tall bar stool with a backrest and footrest
